input.instructions: Derive from first principles the probability that two chords intersect given the constraint that their endpoints must lie in different quadrants. Define the sample space as the set of all possible pairs of quadrants chosen for the endpoints of two chords. For any two chords, identify the specific configurations of quadrant assignments (e.g., chord 1 in quadrants A and B, chord 2 in quadrants C and D) that allow for an intersection. Calculate the probability P that any two randomly selected chords intersect, assuming endpoints are chosen uniformly within their respective quadrants.

variables.selected_prompt_text:
Solve the question directly.
Keep the entire JSON compact.
Keep decision_notes extremely short. Prefer 3 to 8 words.
Do not narrate your reasoning outside the response field.

JUSTIFICATION: <brief justification>
Keep the justification to 12 words or fewer.
Use plain text only inside the response field. Do not use markdown formatting, code fences, or math-style backslashes such as \equiv.
Do not add any extra text before or after the JSON object.

Return exactly one JSON object in this format:
{

  "decision_notes": "optional string",

}

Question:
Derive from first principles the probability that two chords intersect given the constraint that their endpoints must lie in different quadrants. Define the sample space as the set of all possible pairs of quadrants chosen for the endpoints of two chords. For any two chords, identify the specific configurations of quadrant assignments (e.g., chord 1 in quadrants A and B, chord 2 in quadrants C and D) that allow for an intersection. Calculate the probability P that any two randomly selected chords intersect, assuming endpoints are chosen uniformly within their respective quadrants.

results.content:
FINAL_ANSWER: 1/3
JUSTIFICATION: Intersection occurs in 2 out of 6 valid quadrant pairings.
